text:
Once upon a time in ancient Greece, there lived a humble bard named Audax. His favorite thing in the world was collecting stories from travelers. He'd sit in his cozy cottage by the sea and listen until his ears hurt.

One day, a traveling minstrel passed through the village. Audax instantly fell in love with her voice.

"I've heard nothing like your songs!" he said.

For weeks, Audax followed the minstrel. She sang everywhere. Fields. Forests. Mountains.

One day, she stopped beside him on a hill overlooking the ocean. She wasn't singing.

"You have a song hidden there," Audax said.

Inside a bird's nest. She warbled something beautiful. Audax gathered all her courage.

"There," he said. "That was amazing."

Each afternoon afterward, they sang together. Audax's songs about trees. Her songs about sunflowers. Together, they shaped an entire world.

Years went by.

One day, Audax realized
